mission_anchor: Fully implement the single-daemon lifecycle upgrade for pi-code-index so it uses a persistent repo registry, supports automatic idle shutdown, unloads disabled repo runtimes immediately, and exposes daemon-wide observability through doctor/status flows without introducing a new public command surface.
goal: Use registry-backed truth to unload disabled repo runtimes immediately, perform daemon-wide idle detection with graceful auto-shutdown, and avoid unnecessary daemon wakeups by consulting registry hints first in extension startup/tool gating paths.
acceptance_criteria: `/index disable` flips persistent enabled state, stops queued/background work for that repo, unloads its runtime immediately, and preserves on-disk baseline/overlay/cache data.

When no repos remain enabled and there are no active requests or jobs, the daemon enters an idle grace period and then exits cleanly, removing socket and pid artifacts without launchd/systemd helpers.

Extension before_agent_start and any status/tool gating path that only needs enabled/disabled hints can consult durable registry data first so disabled or never-enabled repos do not wake the daemon unnecessarily.
Deterministic tests cover disable-triggered unload, idle auto-shutdown, clean restart after shutdown, and no-wakeup behavior for disabled or never-enabled repos where registry hints are sufficient.
why_now: These behaviors depend on slice 1 making persistent registration authoritative and separating loaded runtimes from registry state.
locked_notes: Disable must preserve cache data by default.
Do not expand beyond macOS/Linux POSIX local transport assumptions.
implementation_surfaces: src/daemon/sqlite-store.ts or new daemon registry module
